behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: failure appears at the boundary between PR-changed-file collection and analyzer input selection, not in a single pure function.
Lower-level miss-proof: a Unit test of filter logic can pass while orchestration still feeds the analyzer an unfiltered file set.
Higher levels rejected by behavior text: no requirement for real OS/network/subprocess timing behavior, and no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire changed-file provider + analysis orchestrator + analyzer with in-process fakes; feed a mixed file set (changed and unchanged) and assert analyzer is invoked only for changed paths.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: grouping correctness depends on handoff from linter findings model to comment renderer/poster payload.
Lower-level miss-proof: a Unit test on formatter alone can miss schema/field mismatches that break grouped output in the posted comment body.
Higher levels rejected by behavior text: posting can be validated via captured client payload; no real network/OS requirement and no AI model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run findings aggregator + comment formatter + PR comment client adapter with a fake GitHub client; provide mixed-severity findings and assert posted body has correct severity sections and membership.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is runtime/performance under real process execution and wall-clock constraints similar to GitHub Actions. Mocked timing or fully in-process stubs can hide I/O/process overhead and timeout behavior. Higher levels rejected by behavior text: no AI/ML decision quality and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step end-to-end as a real process against a generated 200-file PR-like workspace and measure elapsed wall time with a 10-minute threshold; fail if timeout budget is exceeded.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is subprocess crash handling and continuation behavior after process failure. Mocking the linter process can mask real crash semantics (exit codes/signals/partial output). Higher levels rejected by behavior text: no dependence on AI model outputs or chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline with a real linter subprocess where one crafted malformed file triggers a crash; assert supervisor continues with remaining files and still posts a PR comment containing surviving findings plus crash notice/handling output.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: dedup depends on how multiple rule outputs are merged and normalized before output.
Lower-level miss-proof: a Unit test of dedup key logic can pass while upstream rule adapters emit slightly different shapes that bypass dedup in assembled output.
Higher levels rejected by behavior text: no real OS/network/subprocess necessity and no AI/ML call.
behaviors.test_description: Compose two rule-result producers plus normalization/aggregation pipeline in-process; emit overlapping findings for same file/line from different rules and assert final output contains exactly one deduplicated finding.
